observation.summary: Meta-DKS's cold-start guard (below 20 cycles, zero proposals) is intended to avoid schema edits from noise, but 20 cycles is an unvalidated minimum — whether the threshold should scale with vault size, corpus stability, or Toulmin-failure-distribution entropy is unresolved.

warrant.claim: The 20-cycle cold-start threshold, though empirically unvalidated, should be retained and only cautiously revised through a pre-specified external adjudication process, because any revision mechanism that operates below the threshold it is revising is structurally self-undermining.
warrant.data: Meta-DKS's cold-start guard prohibits schema edits when cycle count is below 20 and proposals are zero. The threshold's justification is avoidance of noise-driven schema corruption. The threshold value itself is unvalidated and its proper scaling basis—vault size, corpus stability, Toulmin-failure-distribution entropy, or some combination—is unresolved.
warrant.warrant: Any formal system that asserts the legitimacy of its own internal layer-boundaries must explicitly invoke an external or metatheoretic criterion that is not itself derived solely from the system's own topology. A layered-separation claim is epistemically valid if and only if the criterion used to validate the partition is grounded in a metatheoretic frame that is (i) explicitly distinguished from the object system, (ii) not derived solely from that system's internal axioms or topology, and (iii) acknowledged as provisional relative to any higher metatheoretic frame that may in turn be required.
warrant.backing: If the cold-start guard is revised by a process that itself runs inside Meta-DKS—counting cycles, evaluating proposals, or consulting corpus statistics accumulated under the very threshold under review—then the revision mechanism is an instance of the object system adjudicating its own boundary condition. This violates the Level-Separation Condition: no inferential mechanism may serve simultaneously as the object of a meta-claim and the evaluator of that meta-claim's validity. Furthermore, because the system cannot self-certify the independence of its own boundary conditions without external or structurally heterogeneous anchoring, any internally generated proposal to change the threshold inherits the epistemic taint of the unvalidated threshold it seeks to replace. The conservative posture—preserve the existing guard while external validation is sought—avoids compounding an acknowledged empirical gap with a structurally circular revision.
warrant.qualifier: Presumptively and until an adjudication process anchored outside Meta-DKS's own cycle-counting and schema-evaluation machinery has been established and formally chartered; the threshold remains operative but must be flagged as a pragmatic posit, not a validated optimum, and must carry an explicit live-revisability condition naming the class of external evidence that would trigger renegotiation.

argument_a.evidence: The observation itself concedes that 20 cycles is an unvalidated minimum and that the scaling basis is unresolved—meaning the system currently holds no internally generated evidence that the threshold is correct. Absent positive internal evidence of correctness, and given that any internal measurement of threshold adequacy would be taken within the regime the threshold governs, the epistemic burden falls on proponents of revision to supply externally anchored validation data before the guard is altered. No such data is cited in the observation, confirming that the preconditions for legitimate revision are not yet met.

warrant.claim: The cold-start guard's fixed threshold of 20 cycles is structurally self-undermining because the evidentiary environment it creates cannot produce the kind of evidence that would justify revising it, making any appeal to 'no harm observed during cold-start' epistemically void.
warrant.data: The cold-start guard suppresses all schema-edit proposals during its active window (cycles 0–19). This means the very period during which threshold-calibration evidence would be most diagnostic—early cycles with high noise and potentially high signal—is also the period from which no proposal outcomes are recorded. The guard's operational design forecloses the detection pathway for the class of errors it is meant to prevent (premature edits) as well as the class it may introduce (over-suppression of valid signals). After 20 cycles, any retrospective audit finds only an absence of proposals, not an absence of proposal-worthiness.
warrant.warrant: A valid 'no evidence of harm' inference requires that the evidentiary environment be epistemically open: harm, if present, must have a realistic pathway to detection and recording independent of the policy's own operational control. Where the policy itself narrows or forecloses that pathway, the null finding is endogenous to the design and carries no confirmatory weight. In such cases the burden of proof inverts: defenders of the policy must first demonstrate that the detection environment is sufficiently open before appealing to absence-of-evidence.
warrant.backing: The warrant is reinforced by the complementary observation that uncertainty-management strategies must carry live revisability conditions specifying what evidence would trigger reclassification. The cold-start guard, as currently specified, names no such condition tied to vault size, corpus stability, or Toulmin-failure-distribution entropy. This means its 20-cycle boundary has hardened from a provisional epistemic scaffold into a de facto ontological category ('pre-epistemic period'), which the framework's own principles flag as illegitimate. Additionally, the principle that a warrant imposing boundary conditions must be reflexively inert or equipped with a finite regress-termination criterion applies here: the guard adjudicates its own adequacy by operating through the silence it enforces, a structural circularity that disqualifies it from self-certification.
warrant.qualifier: This argument holds under the assumption that DKS treats post-hoc silence as neutral or mildly confirmatory rather than explicitly marking it as epistemically void; if the system already quarantines cold-start null-findings from any confirmatory role, the circularity is partially mitigated—though the absence of a scaling criterion tied to vault size or entropy remains unresolved.

argument_b.evidence: The observation states directly that 20 cycles is an unvalidated minimum and that whether the threshold should scale with vault size, corpus stability, or Toulmin-failure-distribution entropy is unresolved. No procedural mechanism exists within the current specification that would (a) log suppressed-but-potentially-valid proposals as boundary artifacts for later audit, (b) distinguish noise-driven silence from signal-driven silence during the cold-start window, or (c) activate a review trigger when contextual parameters (vault size, entropy) diverge from implicit baseline assumptions. The guard therefore satisfies neither the 'epistemically open detection environment' condition nor the 'live revisability condition' requirement, leaving its 20-cycle threshold without legitimate empirical or structural grounding.

contradicts.reason: Claim mismatch: A asserts 'The 20-cycle cold-start threshold, though empirically unvalidated, should be retained and only cautiously revised through a pre-specified external adjudication process, because any revision mechanism that operates below the threshold it is revising is structurally self-undermining.'; B asserts "The cold-start guard's fixed threshold of 20 cycles is structurally self-undermining because the evidentiary environment it creates cannot produce the kind of evidence that would justify revising it, making any appeal to 'no harm observed during cold-start' epistemically void."

counter.counter_claim: The attacked warrant assumes that a metatheoretic criterion can be 'explicitly distinguished' and 'not derived solely from the system's internal axioms,' but the cold-start guard's 20-cycle threshold demonstrates that any such supposedly external criterion is itself embedded in the very operational environment it purports to evaluate—making the clean metatheoretic/object-system distinction the warrant relies upon structurally unavailable, not merely unacknowledged.
counter.reason: The attacked warrant requires that a legitimating criterion be (i) explicitly external, (ii) not derived from the system's own topology, and (iii) provisionally acknowledged. The attacker's claim exposes that the 20-cycle threshold is self-sealing: the evidentiary record it generates is constitutively shaped by the threshold itself (only post-cold-start evidence is treated as valid), so any 'external' metatheoretic criterion invoked to validate the partition is in practice constructed from data that the partition itself has pre-filtered. This collapses condition (ii)—the supposed external criterion is covertly derived from the system's own topology—and thereby breaks the warrant's claim that a genuinely independent metatheoretic grounding is achievable. The warrant's logical backbone (that external and internal levels can be cleanly separated) is undercut by the structural feedback loop the cold-start guard creates.

pattern.description: Any criterion positioned as 'external' or 'metatheoretic' with respect to a system is itself instantiated within some operational substrate, and that substrate's concrete parameters (thresholds, cycle counts, initialization conditions) inevitably encode normative and evaluative commitments indistinguishable in kind from those the criterion was meant to adjudicate from outside. The contradiction therefore reveals a structural regularity: the boundary between meta-level and object-level is not a fixed architectural feature but a contextually negotiated artifact—one that collapses whenever the supposed external criterion is traced to its own conditions of possibility, exposing it as another internal rule operating at a different scope.

revised_warrant.claim: No criterion can function as a genuinely external or metatheoretic adjudicator of a system, because every such criterion is itself instantiated within an operational substrate whose concrete parameters encode normative commitments of the same kind as those the criterion purports to evaluate from outside.
revised_warrant.data: Wherever a putative meta-level rule has been traced to its conditions of possibility—threshold values, initialization states, cycle counts, training objectives, institutional protocols—those conditions are found to carry evaluative content functionally equivalent to the object-level rules they were meant to transcend; the boundary therefore reconstitutes itself at a new scope rather than being eliminated.
revised_warrant.warrant: Because the meta/object boundary is a contextually negotiated artifact rather than a fixed architectural feature, any analytic or normative framework must treat its own adjudicative criteria as internal rules operating at a temporarily elevated scope, subject to the same scrutiny as object-level rules; a criterion earns provisional authority not by claiming external standpoint but by making its own substrate parameters explicit, declaring the scope within which it applies, and specifying the conditions under which it yields to a higher-scope revision cycle—thereby preventing the contradition that arises when implicit substrate commitments are mistaken for neutral, transcendent standards.
revised_warrant.backing: Philosophy of science (Neurath's boat; no view from nowhere), reflexive sociology of knowledge (Bourdieu on field theory applied to itself), computational systems theory (meta-interpreters are themselves interpreted), and formal logic (Tarski's hierarchy of truth predicates, each level requiring its own semantic ascent with attendant commitments) all converge on the structural regularity that self-application of a criterion exposes its own conditionedness; the revision rule therefore generalizes across epistemic, computational, and institutional domains.
revised_warrant.qualifier: This warrant holds in all cases where a criterion's substrate can in principle be inspected; it does not entail that all criteria are equally arbitrary—relative stability, explicitness of scope, and revisability under declared conditions distinguish better-functioning internal rules from worse ones, preserving normative differentiation without requiring an impossible external standpoint.
